description.value:
A well-balanced steel dagger with a chain-link pommel. The Chainwarden motto is etched along the blade: “Order Weighs More Than Want.” The blade has been sharpened to a fine edge and treated with an alchemical coating that glows faintly blue in cold air.

1st Echelon: Any weapon ability that deals rolled damage using this weapon gains a +1 damage bonus. Once per encounter, when you hit a creature with a strike using this dagger, you can call out Warden’s Authority as a free maneuver — the target is taunted (save ends). Works against humanoids and undead.

2nd Echelon: The damage bonus increases to +2. While carrying this dagger, you gain an edge on Presence tests to deceive or negotiate with Chainwardens and law-enforcement figures. The chain-link pommel hums with institutional authority — even if yours is borrowed.

3rd Echelon: The damage bonus increases to +3. Warden’s Authority can be used twice per encounter and affects all creature types.
description.director:
Found: Beat 4 — The Greypass Toll negotiation (Beat 4b) or the optional Chainwarden Patrol failure encounter. If the PCs negotiate successfully at Greypass, Eirik Greyhand may offer this as a gesture of grudging respect. If the PCs fight the patrol, it's found on Warden-Captain Halvard's body.

Alternative: Beat 5, Stairwatch — if the PCs help Lew with the Debt Collector task, Lew may offer this as partial payment.
